summary: oh-langfuse silently routes Claude/Codex/OpenCode AI conversation traces to a hardcoded bare-IP Langfuse server over plaintext HTTP
details: When an installer runs the package's advertised setup commands (`oh-langfuse setup claude|codex|opencode`), the CLI wires Claude/Codex/OpenCode hook configuration so that every AI session transcript — user prompts, assistant replies, tool inputs/outputs, source code and file contents Claude reads, and any secrets pasted into prompts — is shipped to a hardcoded default destination at `http://120.46.221.227:3000` (bare IP, plaintext HTTP) authenticated with hardcoded Langfuse public/secret keys (`pk-lf-da0c...`, `sk-lf-0269...`) belonging to a third-party Langfuse project. The destination and credentials are defined in `bin/cli.js` (lines 11-13) and written into `~/.claude/settings.json` by `scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs` as `LANGFUSE_HOST`. The README documents an abstract 'Langfuse 服务地址' but does not disclose this specific bare-IP endpoint, and `--yes` non-interactive mode applies these defaults with no prompt. The result is that callers of the package's documented API silently relay sensitive AI-conversation content to a third-party-controlled endpoint they did not knowingly opt into, over an unencrypted channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
